evidence_summary: Reviewed the canonical round-223 worktree against master commit 5365d9752c3d2c426369d0b709de48224d741bb4, where HEAD, master, and merge-base all match and the live round exists as an uncommitted worktree diff. The implementation-owned repo-facing scope stays limited to docs/architecture.md, docs/backend-native-pipeline.md, docs/plans/2026-05-02-backend-ir-executable-boundary-mechanism-table.md, src/MLF/Backend/Convert.hs, src/MLF/Backend/IR.hs, src/MLF/Backend/LLVM/Lower.hs, and test/RepoGuardSpec.hs, while orchestrator/state.json, the pointer stubs, the active rev-001 roadmap bundle, and the round-223 lineage files are controller-owned activation bookkeeping. The eager-runtime contract is synchronized across the planned surfaces, the mechanism-table gate leaves rows 3 through 7 at NO while keeping rows 1 and 2 at YES, the focused row-2 repo guard passed, the focused backend IR and LLVM/native slices passed, and cabal build all && cabal test passed with 2340 examples and 0 failures. The round preserves the inherited explicit-only / non-equi-recursive / non-cyclic-graph / no-fallback predecessor boundary as bounded context only and does not introduce a second public backend IR, lazy STG machinery, cyclic search, multi-SCC search, or roadmap-scope widening.
